title: Eligibility verdict uses 3-state vocabulary: Eligible / Ineligible / Not yet calculated
description: When a form contains an eligibility verdict or all-conditions-met derivation, the verdict field uses a 3-state result vocabulary instead of a 2-state Yes/No. DEFAULT VOCABULARY (override-able per study): 'Eligible' (all inclusion criteria met AND all exclusion criteria absent), 'Ineligible' (at least one inclusion criterion missed OR at least one exclusion criterion present), 'Not yet calculated' (verdict cannot be determined yet — unanswered conditions remain). DEFAULT CALC STRUCTURE (matches IE-9 conventions): if(<all_eligible_clauses_joined_by_and>, 'Eligible', if(<any_disqualifier_clauses_joined_by_or>, 'Ineligible', 'Not yet calculated')). CALC FORMATTING per IE-9b/c: no spaces around = in expressions (${IEINC1}='Y', not ${IEINC1} = 'Y'); multi-line \n formatting between conditions when calc has more than ~3 individual condition clauses. IMPLEMENTATION PATTERN: a pair of fields — {FORM}ELIG_CALC (type calculate, returns the verdict text), {FORM}ELIG (type text, readonly=yes, calculation=${{FORM}ELIG_CALC}). Both tagged library_source=PROTOCOL_EXTENSION, completion_status=FLAGGED. UNIVERSAL SCOPE per §0.
rationale: A 2-state pattern (Eligible / Not Eligible) would display 'Not Eligible' before any conditions are answered — clinically misleading because eligibility hasn't been determined yet, just unanswered. 3-state cleanly distinguishes 'all conditions evaluated, verdict reached' from 'evaluation incomplete'. This prevents coordinators from acting on a false 'Not Eligible' verdict during data entry, which would otherwise cause unnecessary screen-failure conclusions and rework when the missing conditions get answered. The paired field pattern (calculate field + readonly text field referencing it) makes the verdict both computed and visible to the data-entry user without exposing the underlying expression.

flag.message: Form ${form.form_id} captures eligibility — verify the verdict uses 3-state vocabulary (Eligible / Ineligible / Not yet calculated) via paired fields {FORM}ELIG_CALC (calculate) + {FORM}ELIG (text, readonly, calculation=${{FORM}ELIG_CALC}). 2-state Yes/No is FORBIDDEN — would display 'Not Eligible' before conditions are answered, misleading coordinators.

effect.soft: Emit two paired fields at the end of the form: (1) {FORM}ELIG_CALC type=calculate with expression `if(<inclusion-checks-joined-by-and>, 'Eligible', if(<exclusion-checks-joined-by-or>, 'Ineligible', 'Not yet calculated'))` per IE-9 calc structure; (2) {FORM}ELIG type=text, readonly=yes, calculation=${{FORM}ELIG_CALC} for display. Tag both with library_source=PROTOCOL_EXTENSION and completion_status=FLAGGED. Apply IE-9b/c formatting: no spaces around = in expressions; multi-line \n between conditions when there are more than ~3 clauses. Surface in conventions_applied.eligibility_verdict_3state.{applied_forms, calc_field_pattern}.

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §25 during Phase B.1d. Complements OC-7 sub-rule 7G (clinical_patterns.eligibility_fixed_value_constraints) — 7G handles per-criterion field constraints (.=\'Y\' for inclusion criteria); §25 handles the DERIVED OVERALL VERDICT field that summarizes all criteria. Different fields, different concerns, both apply. Hybrid because field-pair emission (calculate + text-readonly with calculation reference) and the dynamic expression construction (joining variable numbers of inclusion/exclusion clauses) aren't current DSL effect primitives. A future 'emit_field_pair' directive or template-expression operator would promote this to structured.
